spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates document.activeViewTransition flipping from null to a live ViewTransition object during startViewTransition() and back, which is the property's spec contract.

interactive.rationale: Three real buttons (swap, swap-fast, reset) drive an actual startViewTransition() call, a live ViewTransition object readout, and a per-frame poll of the property — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb promises 'watch document.activeViewTransition flip from null to a live object and back' and the page delivers exactly that with a tile, a phase readout, and a double-click skip.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers double-click skip and a no-support fallback, but never demos element-scoped element.activeViewTransition, the AbortError raised when a second transition stomps the first, or the rejected ready/finished promise path.

references.rationale: The 'see also' section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5067126381215744 directly.

design_system.rationale: All colours read from --bg-*, --text-*, --accent-* CSS variables, no static-card pattern, and the warning banner uses the design system's accent-amber + thin-shadow combo.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate element.activeViewTransition (scoped transitions)
openQuestions.detail: css-view-transitions-2 splits the property into document-level and element-level surfaces — element.startViewTransition() updates element.activeViewTransition only, not document.activeViewTransition. The page currently shows just the document property; adding a second tile with element-scoped startViewTransition() and a side-by-side readout would make the spec's two-axis design visible.

openQuestions.title: Surface the AbortError when a second transition stomps the first
openQuestions.detail: If startViewTransition() is called while one is already running, the running one is skipped with AbortError on its ready/finished promises. The current 'swap fast' branch returns early instead of calling through — it should also include a 'force-overlap' mode that calls startViewTransition twice in a row and logs the AbortError so authors see the real abort path the spec describes.

openQuestions.title: Show the phase machine, not just running/idle
openQuestions.detail: ViewTransition has a real phase enum (pending-capture → update-callback-called → animating → done). The page collapses these into 'idle'/'running'. A small phase tracker driven by ready/updateCallbackDone/finished would teach the lifecycle, not just the boolean.

summary: Solid concept that lands the headline contract (null → ViewTransition → null) with a real transition, real polling, and a clear readout. It under-sells the spec by ignoring element-scoped transitions and the AbortError on overlap — both worth a small follow-up.
